name: Empress Zhang [Xingcai]

novel_notes:
Differences Between Fact and Common Fiction
	
		
	Outside mention of her marriage to Liu Shan, stories of Empress Zhang, particularly those of her fighting, tend to be fictional.

Traditional Chinese

	 Simplified Chinese

	 Pronunciation

	 Pinyin (Tones) 
	Zhānghuánghòu [xīngcǎi]


	 Wade-Giles 
	Empress Chang [Hsingts‘ai]

	 Birthplace 
	Zhuo commandery

	 Other Names 
	Lady Zhang, Zhang Xingcai, Zhang Shi

	 Name Notes 
	Given a fictional personality by Koei under the fictional name of ‘Zhang Xingcai’ or simply ‘Xing Cai’. Both names refer to the same person, but the story given her in Koei games is fictional. I’ve recorded this as the English translation presents itself (so, Huanghou Zhang (empress Zhang) and Xing1cai3 as her given name)

sections.biography-collaborative-works:
Zhang Fei’s eldest daughter. Became Empress to Liu Shan (Empress Jing’ai). Her mother was Lady Xiahou, cousin to Xiahou Ba. When Liu Shan became Emperor, according to the advice of Zhuge Liang and others, Lady Zhang became his Empress at the age of seventeen. In the year AD 221, Lady Zhang entered the court as the consort of the Crown Prince Liu Shan. In AD 223, she became Empress. She died in AD 237. The title of Empress passed down to her younger sister. (1)

(1) Adapted from Romance of the Three Kingdoms XI.

full_text:
Empress Zhang [Xingcai] 張皇后[星彩]
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Empress Zhang [Xingcai] (張皇后[星彩])was born in Zhuo commandery. Eldest daughter of Zhang Fei, became the wife of Liu Shan. Empress Zhang [Xingcai] was affiliated with the Shu Kingdom. Empress Zhang [Xingcai] lived during the Three Kingdoms period of Chinese history.

Empress Zhang’s SGZ
BiographyCollaborative Works
Zhang Fei’s eldest daughter. Became Empress to Liu Shan (Empress Jing’ai). Her mother was Lady Xiahou, cousin to Xiahou Ba. When Liu Shan became Emperor, according to the advice of Zhuge Liang and others, Lady Zhang became his Empress at the age of seventeen. In the year AD 221, Lady Zhang entered the court as the consort of the Crown Prince Liu Shan. In AD 223, she became Empress. She died in AD 237. The title of Empress passed down to her younger sister. (1)
(1) Adapted fromRomance of the Three Kingdoms XI.
